Table 3 Myocardial blood flow in subepicardial and subendocardial septum

Key:$\mathrm{Symprotons=change}$ in symptomatic status after verapamil;$ A=improved$ |$(n=8)$ ;$\mathrm{B}=\mathrm{worseneed}$ or no change $(n=6);\mathrm{dp}=\mathrm{dpyridamole};$ ; bas =li;la respectively;$\mathrm{CFR}=\mathrm{coronary}$ flow reserve (dipyridamole/baseline myocardial blood flow).All values of myocardial blood low are in ml/min/g

Fig. 3 Values of myocardial blood flow (MBF) are shown for subendocardial (endo) and subepicardial (epi) septal regions before and after treatment in patients that improved symptomatically following drug therapy (group A) and in those that reported no change or a worsening of symptoms (group B).All values represent mean + 1standard deviation.
